title: The Frozen Stream
content:
Stream that leapt and danced
    Down the rocky ledges,
    All the summer long,
    Past the flowered sedges,
    Under the green rafters,
    With their leafy laughters,
    Murmuring your song:
    Strangely still and tranced,
    All your singing ended,
    Wizardly suspended,
    Icily adream;
    When the new buds thicken,
    Can this crystal quicken,
    Now so strangely sleeping,
    Once more go a-leaping
    Down the rocky ledges,
    All the summer long,
    Murmuring its song?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Richard Le Gallienne delivers a powerful performance in "The Frozen Stream"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
